content.full_text:
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, California 95814 
Gentlemen: 
October 24, 1984 
Enclosed please find an excerpt from the October, 1984 Western City issue 
relative to Officeholders not involved in an Election. 
I request a clarification on this matter. 
Our Town Council consists of five 
members 
four Councilmembers elected for four year terms, and a Mayor elected 
for a two year term. 
Other than election reporting requirements, I have only 
had the Councilmembers file an annual report for the period of taking office 
~hrough uecember 31st or January 1st through December 31st, which ever is 
applicable. 
As of July 1, 1984, the five member Council receives a salary of $150.00 per 
month each. 
Does this mean that they will now have to file a "semi-annual" 
campaign statement? 
Non of my Councilmembers have mid election funds to 
report. 
If this form is required, what is the Form No. and what would be the 
period covered? 
Would they still be required to file annual disclosure 
statements? 
Your assistance in this matter will be greatly appreciated. 
11/9/84--Called, left message. 
for three weeks. 
She's on vacation 
12/3--Discussed filing procedures for 
not being voted on. 
She was confused 
721 and campaign filings. 
C. Wardlow 
Since,rely,

ning with the day after the closing date

If 
you are involved in the November dcc-

old statement, if you raise or spend $500 
or more during a threshold period. 
Important Information about 
"Late Contributions" 
October 21 
through November 5, 1984. is the "late 
contribution" period for the November 
6th General Election. A late contribution 
is any contribution. including a loan, ag-
gregating $1,000 or more during the late 
contribution period from a single source 
that is made to or received by a candi-
date, controlled committee, Of commit-
tee primarily formed to support or 
oppose a candidate or measure being 
voted upon. Both recipients and the can-
didate or committee making the late con-
tribution must file reports within 48 
hours of either receiving or making the 
latc contribution. A laIC contribution 
report must be sent by te 
ram, 
mailgram, any overnight guaranteed 
delivery service or by personal delivery

volved in an April 9, 1985 Election 
Thl' following filing schedule is for can-
didates and committees involved in an 
April 9, 1985, election. There are no 
"thre~h()lds" for statements filed in con-
nection with elections held on dates other 
than the June or November state election 
dates. Pre- and post-election campaign 
statements must be filed during each 
period, regardless of whether there has 
been any reportable activity. If you an-
ticipate receiving or spending less than 
$500 on your entire campaign, you may 
avoid filing pre- and post-election state-
ments 
filing a Form 470 with your 
Declaration of Candidacy or filing the 
Form 470 on or before the deadline for 
the first pre-election campaign state-
ment. The period covered by the Form 
470 would be January I, 1985, through 
June 30, 1985. Candidates who antici-
pate raising or spending $500 or more 
on behalf of your candidacy should not 
use the Form 470, but file according to 
the election schedule for the April 9 
election. 
March 24 through April 8 is the "late 
contribution" period for an April 9th

sections.question: Does this mean that they will now have to file a "semi-annual" campaign statement? Non of my Councilmembers have mid election funds to report. If this form is required, what is the Form No. and what would be the period covered? Would they still be required to file annual disclosure statements?
sections.question_synthetic: The Town Clerk of Yountville seeks clarification on whether council members receiving $150/month salary are now required to file semi-annual campaign statements under new FPPC guidelines, and if so, what form number and reporting periods apply.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response/advice letter. No formal CONCLUSION or SHORT ANSWER section provided. Internal FPPC notes dated 11/9/84 and 12/3/84 indicate staff follow-up but no formal response text is included in this document. The enclosed Western City article discusses filing requirements for officeholders not involved in elections but does not constitute an FPPC formal response.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request from the Town Clerk of Yountville, California, seeking clarification on FPPC filing requirements for five-member town council members whose salaries increased to $150/month as of July 1, 1984. The request includes a Western City article excerpt about officeholder filing requirements and internal FPPC notes from follow-up calls and discussions.
